description: Named after the French author who came from Provence, this wine is a bright, fruity style with pepper and caramel flavors. It has plenty of red fruits and attractive final acidity.
title: La Belle Collection 2015 Colette Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Produced from organically grown grapes, this is the top of Troteligotte range. It is made from 25-year-old vines, and aged in amphorae. That gives a wine that is rich with soft rounded tannins and juicy blackberry fruits. It has a dense texture from aging on the lees in the amphorae. Rich and concentrated it will age and should not be drunk until 2020.
title: Clos Troteligotte 2014 K-2 Malbec (Cahors)

description: Doussot is a producer in the Aube region in southern Champagne, which explains the high alcohol for Champagne. However the wine still has all the crisp, mineral character of Champagne, broadened out with pineapple and ripe orange flavors. It's a youthful wine, showing tight acidity and that would soften and round in 3–4 more years.
title: Veuve Doussot 2008 Ernestine Brut  (Champagne)

description: From a very good if not great Champagne vintage, this mature wine is delicious and beautifully ripe. It still has plenty of crisp acidity, allowing the fruit to shine. At the same time, a yeasty, toasty character indicates its gentle aging. After eight years in the cellar before disgorging, it is ready to drink.
title: Boude Baudin 2007 Cuvée Amour de B  (Champagne)

description: Ripe fruit and balanced acidity allow this wine to make room for touches of wood aging. It has spice, a smoky character and attractive finishing freshness. Drink from 2017 when the wine will be fully balanced.
title: Château Saintongey 2014 Vieilles Vignes  (Bordeaux)

description: Still very young, this is a Maison Bouey wine with good potential. It offers hints of the ripe yellow fruits and richness that will come as it develops. The touch of perfumed spice means it is also a wine to savor as an apéritif.
title: Château Lestruelle 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Produced from a small acre of land in Margaux, this is made in the same extravagant style as the cult wines of Saint-Emilion. With 100% new wood, it is definitely modern in style, but there is sufficient concentration in the fruit to balance all that vanilla. It misses the 2006 freshness,but everything else is there.
title: Château Cambon la Pelouse 2006 L'Aura  (Margaux)

description: This rosé is warm and soft, with an attractive perfume and lively acidity to balance. Its red berry fruit is finished with a touch of spice that gives a final lift to the smooth vanilla-inflected aftertaste.
title: Florent Descombe NV Syrah Rosé (Vin de France)

description: Dark pink in color, this full wine offers tannins as well as crisp red fruits, giving it structure and a firm texture. The wine blends nearly all Bordeaux grapes: Malbec, Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. Drink it chilled, like a rosé.
title: Château Landereau 2014  Bordeaux Clairet

description: Textured, mineral and tight wine, its crisp acidity a refreshing feature. It's very fresh, terroir-driven, a wine that shows its origins right up front. Apples and citrus add to the bright, fruity nature of this wine.
title: Cave des Grands Crus Blancs 2012 Minéral  (Mâcon-Loché)

description: The producer is following the Provence fashion and packaging its range of Pure Loire in square bottles. This rosé is dry with red fruit and spice from the Cabernet Franc in the blend. Tangy and crisp, it is ready to drink,
title: Bougrier 2015 Pure Loire  (Rosé de Loire)

description: From the Côtes de Provence, this is a lightweight sparkling rosé wine that boasts tasty strawberry and sour-apple flavors. A hint of chocolate creeps in on the finish.
designation: Le Grand Cros La Maîtresse Rosé Brut
title: Domaine du Grand Cros NV Le Grand Cros La Maîtresse Rosé Brut Sparkling (Vin Mousseux)

description: A restrained nose only reveals faint hints of baked apple. The palate comes in with ripe red apple and a rather bitter edge of almond. The body is light but balanced while the finish is dry and poised.
title: Henri Schoenheitz NV Brut Auxerrois (Alsace)

description: This is a rich wine, full of juicy fruit and attractive acidity. A blend of 95% Malbec and 5% Merlot, it has touches of wood aging along with the tannins. It will develop quickly, so drink from 2018.
title: Vignobles Arbo 2014 Arbo Malbec  (Côtes de Bordeaux)

description: This wine comes from one parcel that has particularly flinty soil. With a strongly mineral character, it is tight and nervy, needing some time to calm down. The fruit is all there, fresh citrus, apples and green plums, all ready to burst out over the next few months. Drink from 2017.
title: Domaine Paul Thomas 2014 Silex  (Sancerre)

description: No longer a second wine of Ducru-Beaucaillou, but a wine from its own separate vineyard, Croix de Beaucaillou is impressive. Firm while also rich, it is firmly tannic, superripe and packed with great dark plum and berry fruits. Fruit and structure well balanced together.
title: Croix de Beaucaillou 2010  Saint-Julien

description: Here's true “old vine” wine—from vines 75 years old on average—and it is ripe and toasty, with flavors of almonds, spice and baked bread. Fresh, crisp apples and green plums are lifted by a citrus aftertaste. Drink this attractive wine now.
title: Pierre Merguey 2014 Vielles Vignes  (Pouilly-Fuissé)

description: Soft, full and very rounded, this has yellow fruit flavors that are ripe and open, with its acidity emerging slowly. The finish is more focused and fresh.
title: Château Philippe-le-Hardi 2010  Mercurey

description: From one of the estates owned by the Pagès family in the northern Médoc, the wine is spicy and rich with tannins and blackberry fruit. As a contrast, it is shot through with attractive acidity that is a character of the year. The wine will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Château la Roque de By 2014  Médoc

description: So soft, the wine is gently fruity, a hint of its chalk soil through a mineral edge. Fresh and tasting of lime and apple fruits, it is ready to drink now.
title: Domaine L. Chatelain 2010  Chablis

description: This ripe, balanced and mature wine is ready to drink. It has rich flavors of white fruits as well as more tropical hints. A pure line of acidity cuts through the riper fruit flavors and leaves a crisp texture with orange-zest flavor at the end.
title: Aubry NV Le Nombre d'Or Brut  (Champagne)

description: Rich and fruity, this is full of red berry and black plum fruits, sustained by dense, powerful tannins. The wine manages to balance freshness with a solid, dry core.
title: Château du Cèdre 2010 Cèdre Heritage Malbec (Cahors)

description: Aromas of bananas follow through to the palate of this wine, spoiling the structured black plums and tannins. The wine is fresh and juicy, with some dry structure at the background. Drink from 2016.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2014 Domaine des Rosiers  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Meat and bacon aromas introduce a wine that is taut, structured and high on the acidity. It has a lean, slightly dirty taste, which is not attractive.
title: Château Tire Pé 2009  Bordeaux

description: This is significantly young, tight and firm. Coming from one of the best sites in Fleurie, it has structure and tannins that dominate the rich red cherry fruits. Drink from 2016.
title: Domaine Chaintreuil 2013 La Madonne  (Fleurie)

description: Light and fruity, this soft Champagne has red berry fruits, noticeable sweetness and fresh acidity to match. It's attractive for those who like their Champagne soft and easygoing.
title: Vranken NV Diamant Brut Rosé  (Champagne)

description: Drouhin has significant holdings in a number of top Chambolle vineyards. This wine is a blend from other Premier Crus in the same village. It has the typical generous character along with a more serious structured backbone that gives a dry and solid density that's worth aging. Drink from 2017.
title: Joseph Drouhin 2012 Premier Cru  (Chambolle-Musigny)

description: This structured, concentrated wine is from an estate in the north of Saint-Estèphe. A dominance of Cabernet Sauvignon in the blend lends it firm, solid tannins as well as a juicy black-currant note. It's a wine for long-term aging; don't drink before 7–8 years.
title: Château Beau-Site 2010  Saint-Estèphe

description: Very toasty aromas materialize into a wine that has way too much wood aging. There is no sign of fruit here, only wood plank flavors.
title: Château Beaulieu Comtes de Tastes 2011  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: This ripe wine has jammy red fruit flavors. The fruitiness is given structure by the stalky tannins and the dry, wood-aging flavors. Black cherries and juicy black currants show strongly. It needs to age, so drink from 2016.
title: Château la Gordonne 2010 Verité du Terroir Red (Côtes de Provence)

description: A rich hint of apple crumble precedes tart notes of lemon freshness. The palate fizzes with frothy bubbles and gives the impression of lightness and ease. A very clean-cut, sprightly and refreshing finish almost hides the more serious autolytic backdrop of this elegant, fine-fruited Crémant.
title: Boeckel 2014 Blanc de Blancs Brut Auxerrois (Alsace)

description: This wine is fruity and crisp, with attractive balanced acidity. It's all about raspberry and red currants. A refreshing character gives citrus tang to the aftertaste.
title: Baron Philippe de Rothschild 2014 Le Rosé de Mouton Cadet Rosé (Bordeaux Rosé)

description: Typical of early-maturing, Merlot-based wines from this vintage, this is rich, swirling with black-berry fruits and generous tannins. The fresh, almost crisp aftertaste is already attractive. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Dassault 2013  Saint-Émilion

description: This second wine of Lafite Rothschild is packed with acidity, nervy black currant fruits and extreme freshness. It's a light wine, all fruit and only hints of tannin and structure.
title: Château Lafite Rothschild 2013 Les Carruades de Lafite  (Pauillac)

description: Now mature, this wine is full of secondary, dried fruit and spice flavors along with tannins that are now fully evolved. It is rich, definitely ready to drink with its acidity and dry aftertaste. The former owners of Château Montrose in Bordeaux, Anne-Marie et Jean-Louis Charmolüe, purchased this property in 2006. All Les Baux vineyards are biodynamic.
title: Château Romanin 2009 Le Coeur Red (Les Baux de Provence)

description: 92-94 Spice and mint aromas with blackberry fruit, new wood and a polished character. The acidity is delicious, refreshing.
title: Château Faugères 2010 Barrel sample  (Saint-Émilion)

description: Clos Windsbuhl in Hunawihr is the northernmost of Zind-Humbrecht's collection of vineyards. In 2012, it has produced an impressive wine that has all the right spice of Pinot Gris along with acidity and a fresh edge. That gives this rich wine a great lift, with a waft of pear to go with the yellow-fruit flavors. Drink from 2016.
title: Domaine Zind-Humbrecht 2012 Clos Windsbuhl Hunawihr Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: A simple, rounded wine, soft and fruity. Pear and apricot flavors dominate along with a crisper, grapefruit edge. The wine is aging quickly, and will be ready in a few months.
title: Domaine du Chalet Pouilly 2011  Saint-Véran

description: Soft, lilting wine that is gentle, ripe and ready to drink. The fruits have some sweetness, the tannins are light, and the acidity is fresh and never too intrusive.
title: Bouchard Père & Fils 2007  Monthélie

description: While still young and fruity, this has the potential to be a complex, long-lived wine. It boasts stone fruit and white flower aromas, a tight structure and a hint of sweetness. Drink from 2018.
title: Domaine Albert Mann 2011 Furstentum Grand Cru Riesling (Alsace)

description: Although this is not the newest release from Hecht & Bannier, it is drinking nicely now, with mature notes of kirsch and black currant backed by accents of sweet spice, crushed violets and soft leather. Though aged, the mouth is still full and big with gripping tannins and a firm structure. The finish is long, evoking flavors of cocoa-dusted blackberries and peppery spice. Drink now–2014.
title: Hecht & Bannier 2007 Red (Faugères)

description: Citric tartness dominates nose and palate, making for a spritzy, lime-flavored fizz with frothy bubbles. The dry palate is light yet fruity, making this fun, easy-drinking and refreshing.
title: Gustave Lorentz NV Sparkling (Crémant d'Alsace)

description: Just mature, this smooth, velvet-textured wine has juicy black fruits with a smoky character that adds interest to the structure and acidity that give the wine shape. Rich while fresh at the end, it is ready to drink.
title: Cave du Marmandais 2012 La Vieille Eglise Réserve Red (Côtes du Marmandais)

description: This vineyard is at one of the highest Castillon points. Big in tannins and also fruit, this is a dense wine. It has a dry core as well as rich blackberry fruits. Concentrated and full, the wine will be seriously worth drinking from 2019.
title: Château Montlandrie 2014  Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux

description: This old-vine wine shows signs of rotten fruit, certainly aromatically. On the palate, it is tight with dark tannins and a hard, extracted edge. It is hard to tell if it could soften. This property liess in both the Castillon and Franc appellations. Wine is vinified plot by plot.
title: Château Terrasson 2013 Cuvée Prevenche  (Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux)

description: A mineral texture gives this ripe wine an edge while its full, red berry fruit offers some solid tannins and concentration. It's smoky and full-bodied and will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Château Joanin Bécot 2015  Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux

description: This sweet wine balances acidity with honeyed fruit. There is a touch of botrytis to go with the orange marmalade and lemon peel flavors, while the citrus-like acids cut across the palate. This is fine to drink now, yet it will also age for many years.
title: Château Jolys 2010 Cuvée Jean  (Jurançon)

description: This juicy, fruity wine has red berry fruit and gentle tannins. Although ripe, it's not rich but attractive and likely to age quickly. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Pilet 2015 Alt. 120 Metres  (Côtes de Bordeaux)

description: Orange-pink color on this soft, ripe rosé. The balance is better than in past years, with acidity cutting the red fruits. Light-bodied with a good texture.
title: Vranken NV Demoiselle Rosé Brut  (Champagne)

description: Heavy wood aromas are mitigated on the palate by the ripe fruit, making for a rich wine with black plum flavors as well as a core of tannins. It's going to develop well and quickly, so drink from 2016.
title: Château Carcanieux 2012  Médoc

description: This is the best Delamotte vintage since 1996, a ripe and rich wine that brings out the chalky soil character with a strong mineral content. It has weight along with a relatively high dosage that gives softness. It is balanced, fruity and ready to drink.
title: Delamotte 2004 Blanc de Blancs Brut Chardonnay (Champagne)

description: This is a concentrated wine, full with dry tannins and perfumed red fruits. Hints of the wood aging give the wine toast and smokiness. The fresh fruits form a contrast offering a future of fragrance and stylishness. It needs to age, so wait until 2019.
title: Château de Santenay 2012 Montée Rouge Premier Cru  (Beaune)

description: Some funk on the nose soon blows off to reveal generously ripe fruit of yellow pear. The palate benefits from a touch of flavor-boosting residual sweetness but the finish is dry. This is full fruited and balanced. Drink while flavors are at their peak.
title: Frédéric Mallo 2015 Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: Crisp and fruity, this is a wine that hides nothing. It is all upfront, fresh fruit backed with lively acidity. It's a wine that sings brightly and effortlessly.
title: Château Lauduc 2012  Bordeaux Blanc

description: This shows the depth of structure that can be found in a villages-level wine, even when it's a Nouveau. It has weight and spicy fruit, with upfront, juicy cherry and banana flavors.
title: Signé Vigneron 2012 Château du Souzy Nouveau  (Beaujolais-Villages)

description: This Chardonnay is a world-class wine available at a fraction of the cost of other excellent French examples. It boasts soft wood accents to the ripe yellow apple, pear and spiced orange tones, with zippy acidity and a mineral streak that lend freshness to the medium-weight palate. It's a layered, complex wine that will evolve well through 2022.
title: Château Rives-Blanques 2015 Odyssée Chardonnay (Limoux)

description: This blend of Syrah and Grenache has minty, wet-plaster aromas, but also some pure Grenache notes. The palate runs tart, with cranberry and cherry. And the finish is tight and bitter, with more acids than tannin. A viable bistro wine but go no further.
title: Château de Jau 2002 Jaja Red (Vin de Pays d'Oc)

description: The label and the name of this wine just exude summer sun. It is tight, packed with acidity as well as bright red fruits. A citrus streak shoots through this light fruity wine. Drink now.
title: Les Maîtres Vignerons de la Presqu'île de Saint-Tropez 2016 Note Bleue Rosé (Côtes de Provence)
winery: Les Maîtres Vignerons de la Presqu'île de Saint-Tropez

description: Hints of pear perfume the nose on this wine, while the defined palate is bright with lemony freshness. Bundled concentration at the lean, taut core will round out with more bottle age. Drink 2017–2025.
title: Domaine François Schmitt 2014 Grand Cru Pfingstberg Paradis Riesling (Alsace)

description: A charged Burgundy, packed with dense tannins and powerful ripe black berry fruits. There's a delicious tension here between the fruits and the tannin, pushing the acidity, the red plums and strawberries forward, but keeping them in check. At this stage, it needs decanting; otherwise keep for 2–3 years.
title: Joseph Drouhin 2005  Gevrey-Chambertin

description: Crisp, very light and fruity, this is an attractive, ready-to-drink wine. A touch of acidity at the end gives freshness.
title: Les Héritiers du Comte Lafon 2012  Mâcon-Milly Lamartine

description: This wine comes from one of Côtes de Provence historic cru classé estates. It is a ripe and smooth wine with crisp acidity and a fine bite of spice. The wine is packed with red fruits and finished with refreshing acidity.
title: Fabre en Provence 2016 Château de la Clapière Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: This is a smooth wine whose tannins have been already integrated into the blackberry fruits. It is ripe, still young of course, and with a stalky texture in the background. With acidity and fruitiness, the wine needs to age. Drink from late 2017.
title: Château la Barotte 2015 Cuvée Prestige  (Bordeaux)

description: This is a ripe, dry style of rosé. It has a dense texture that is rich with strawberry fruits as well as good acidity and a balanced tangy texture of peeled oranges. The wine is fresh, full of fruit and ready to drink.
title: Collet NV Brut Rosé de Saignée  (Champagne)

description: A light-colored, delicate wine that is smooth and soft. It has a good balance of red-cherry flavors, with a little bitterness, layered over some gentle tannins. Not for aging, but attractively juicy now.
title: Maison Champy 2008  Savigny-lès-Beaune

description: Opulent and ripe, but at the same time it shows red fruits, fresh acids and a structured core. It has a wonderful, generous finish that showcases the rich fruits and warm tannins.
title: Chanson Père et Fils 2009 Clos des Mouches Premier Cru  (Beaune)

description: Made exclusively from Pinot Noir, this is a rich and highly textured wine. Red fruit aromas yield a ripe impression on the palate, while at the same time, it is dry and crisp in tangy acidity, with a zesty lemon flourish. This food-friendly wine is ready to drink.
designation: Grand Cru Brut Blanc de Noirs
title: Besserat de Bellefon NV Grand Cru Brut Blanc de Noirs Pinot Noir (Champagne)

description: This rich, wood-aged wine shows a fine balance between rich black-fruit and new toast flavors. It has weight and power, without being over the top. For medium-term aging.
title: Château la Cabanne 2009  Pomerol

description: This full-bodied wine is packed with citrus and tight, apple-driven acidity, as well as a rich touch of apricot. This fruitiness is balanced by a deliciously refreshing character that creates texture and a steely, minerally feel.
title: René Geoffroy NV Expression Brut  (Champagne)

description: This wine is fresh, crisp and still young. Crisp acidity dominates the strongly citrus character and gives a tight aftertaste of lime and pink grapefruit. This 100% Sauvignon Blanc was aged on the lees for six months to enhance aroma. Wait to drink until early 2016.
title: Dourthe 2014 La Grande Cuvée  (Bordeaux Blanc)

description: A wine with some weight, showing simple fruit and acidity, the texture open and with a high dosage. It is easy, soft and balanced.
title: J. de Telmont NV Grande Réserve Brut  (Champagne)

description: A light, bright, herbaceous wine, this is the epitome of easy-drinking Sauvignon Blanc. Crisp apple and grapefruit flavors dominate the lemon zest texture. Drink now. Screwcap.
title: Henri Bourgeois 2011 Petit Bourgeois Sauvignon Blanc (Val de Loire)

description: A richly smoky wine, with all its juiciness surrounded by dark tannins. Despite a dominance of Merlot, this is still intensely structured. The wine has so much richness as well as acidity.
title: Château Pibran 2009  Pauillac

description: This wine has a bright, crisp character that is reminiscent of Chablis. It has refreshing lemon and apple flavors, and it's ready to drink.
title: Antonin Rodet 2010 Château de Mercey  (Bourgogne Hautes Côtes de Beaune)

description: Made by the winemaking team at Cos d'Estournel, this is a very soft, generous wine that burst with fruit in the mouth. The structure is easy, gently tannic but not more. The pleasure is from the sweet fruit.
title: Goulée 2006  Médoc

description: A blend of Cabernet and Merlot, this has red fruit notes, a light structure and plenty of final acidity. It finishes with caramel as well as freshness.
title: Producteurs Plaimont 2013 Colombelle L'Originel Rosé (Côtes de Gascogne)

description: Generous notes of ripe, juicy pear lead on nose and palate of this wine. An earthy, slightly bitter edge lends definition and texture but undereath it all lies the purity of fresh-cut Conference pear and lemon. This wine is bone-dry and lip-smackingly refreshing.
title: Domaine Ostertag 2014 Barriques Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: While the texture is rich and full-bodied, there is an edge of sharp acidity that takes away from the generous ripeness. Tannins and juicy red fruits are present, but the acidity is a little jarring.
title: Domaine Guy & Yvan Dufouleur 2009 Clos du Chapitre Premier Cru  (Fixin)

description: Oak aging and no filtration give this beautiful wine richness and dense texture. A touch of spicy toast contrasts with the melon and grapefruit flavors, smoothly ripe texture and nutmeg aftertaste. The wine is concentrated and full in the mouth. This opulent wine is still young, so wait until late 2017 to drink.
title: Henri Bourgeois 2014 Sancerre d'Antan Terroir de Silex  (Sancerre)

description: The Lobre family created a herbal wine that is crisp with green fruits, green herbs and tangy acidity. It is fresh, lightly perfumed and with touches of more tropical flavors. The wine could do with a few more months, so drink from late 2016.
title: Château de l'Aubrade 2015  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Tight, young and tangy, this wine will be delicious when it all comes together. The elements are all there, with the quince and pear fruits, intense acidity and the crisp texture that is so refreshing. Drink this ageworthy wine from 2017.
title: Domaine de la Gaverie 2014 Sec  (Vouvray)

description: A light wine, somewhat unfocused and lacking fruit intensity. But it is certainly pleasant, clean and fresh, with a definite green tinge and a citrus aftertaste.
title: Fournier Père et Fils 2006 Fournier  (Menetou-Salon)

description: Crisp raspberry flavors give this Barthe family wine an attractive fruitiness and acidity. It is light on the palate, still tight and needs a few more months. Drink from 2016.
title: Château la Freynelle 2013  Bordeaux

description: Slightly confected cherry notes open onto a rather soft, somewhat dilute palate with friendly, easy fruit. This might work as a refreshing summer red when chilled.
title: Hubert Meyer 2014 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: A perfumed wine, this is full of black fruits that are shot through with acidity. It is bright, soft and only hinting at tannins. This second wine of Château Pichon Longueville Comtesse de Lalande will develop quickly and become an attractive, fruity wine from 2018.
title: Château Pichon Longueville Comtesse de Lalande 2013 Réserve de la Comtesse  (Pauillac)
winery: Château Pichon Longueville Comtesse de Lalande

description: With firm tannins and ripe, balanced fruit, this is a success for the vintage, profiting from the dry conditions during harvest in Saint-Estèphe. Blackberry fruits and rich tannins give the wine shape and aging potential. Keep the wine until 2019.
title: Château Lilian Ladouys 2013  Saint-Estèphe

description: This is classic and fun, bold and fruity, with weight coming from the firm tannins. Pair this with food.
title: Bouchard Aîné & Fils 2012 Nouveau  (Beaujolais)

description: This is an attractively perfumed wine, which is polished by the wood influence, offering flavors of mint and eucalyptus. It is rich and spicy, with the weight of black fruits. This is already drinkable, so consume it now, or keep it for 3–4 years.
title: Château la Levrette 2009  Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Ripe black cherry, raspberry and wild strawberry notes abound on the nose and in the mouth of this accessible wine. Medium weight, with fine yet spicy tannins and a medium-length finish that's kissed with a hint of mocha. Easy, approachable and food friendly; drink now.
title: Les Vignerons de Tautavel Vingrau 2009 Réserve Vieilles Vignes Red (Côtes du Roussillon Villages Tautavel)

description: Firmly tannic, this is a solid, chunky wine. It's full of dark plum, black currant and cherry flavors that fill out the firm, dry structure. Concentrated while also fruity, it's worth aging for a year.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2011 Domaine Moulin à Vent  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Still very closed up, this is going to be a classic. Its tannins are already balanced to the immense juicy fruits and dark wood flavors that bring out spiciness. It's powerful while not over the top, showing elegance at the same time as an opulent texture. Give this gorgeous wine until 2018.
title: Château du Cèdre 2011 GC Malbec (Cahors)

description: A light and very fresh wine. It has layers of citrus, some vanilla and pure acidity that cuts right through to give a refreshing aftertaste.
title: Cave de Lugny 2010 Vieilles Vignes  (Mâcon-Villages)

description: Ripe and fruity, this is a rich style of rosé. It has forward succulent strawberry flavors that are gently lifted by acidity. There is also a young, almost bitter character that, unusually, suggests this rosé needs to age. Drink summer 2016.
title: Pierre Prieur et Fils 2014 Domaine de Saint-Pierre Rosé (Sancerre)

description: The senses are immediately captured by the purity of the perfectly ripe apple notes that rise from the glass. The palate continues in that pure, joyous, juicy vein. This has incredible power and despite its muscle and undoubted weight there is no fat. The palate is dry and streamlined and bouncing with fruit and life. Lovely now, sure to age beautifully. Drink 2017–2030.
title: Domaine Saint-Rémy 2015 Ehrhart Hengst Grand Cru Riesling (Alsace)

description: Mainly Grenache, this is a full, rich, velvety Rhône blend. Toasty oak, vanilla and baking spices dominate the youthful aromas, backed by black cherry fruit on the palate. It's warm and plush on the finish. Drink now–2025.
title: Chateau Les Amoureuses 2012 Terres des Amoureuses Voluptueuse Red (Ardèche)

description: The only question with this wine is whether it might be too much of a good thing. It's full bodieed and densely concentrated, with oaky notes of toasted almond and vanilla layered over plummy fruit, finishing warm and lush. Hedonistic to the max. Drink now.
title: Tardieu-Laurent 2008 Vieilles Vignes Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: The almost legendary wine of Gassac is in fact a deeply satisfying, rich but structured southern wine, with a fine balance between spice, red fruits and powerful tannins. There's a raw edge of youth here, confirming that these wines always have considerable aging potential.
region_1: Vin de Pays de la Haute Vallée du Gassac
title: Mas de Daumas Gassac 2005 Red (Vin de Pays de la Haute Vallée du Gassac)

description: Red berry fruits are right up front in this juicy, fruity wine without great sense of structure. No matter, it's attractive and fresh now, ready to drink.
title: Producta Vignobles 2012  Bordeaux

description: The Sylvaner's essential spice and pepper quality is well brought out in this stylish wine. The pear and quince flavors come through strongly, finishing with acidity.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2009 Trois Châteaux Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: The wine from this estate perched high above Château Angélus is tight and mineral. While the dark dense tannins are to the fore, it also has great fruits. The acidity and the crisp texture give the wine both its intensity and its potential. Drink from 2025.
title: Château Bellevue 2014  Saint-Émilion

description: This is a full, ripe wine that's rounded and rich on the palate. With ripe yellow fruit and tangerine flavors, it's tropical in style, although there is a tangy lemon edge to it to keep it crisp and herbaceous.
title: Patient Cottat 2009 Anciennes Vignes  (Menetou-Salon)

description: The Grenache and Cinsault in this rosé are from the Jura and other vineyards. The result is a soft, smooth and creamy wine. Fresh red-berry fruits give it a round, almost off-dry character. It's fresh at the end, and definitely in an apéritif style.
title: François Montand NV Brut Rosé Sparkling (Vin Mousseux)

description: Rich and warm, this is a big, ripe wine. Toasted marshmallow notes frame honeyed pineapple, while hints of singed fruit and mocha linger on the finish. Drink now.
title: Lavau 2012 White (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: 87–89. Barrel sample. Spicy wood comes through strongly, giving this wine a bitterness that's contrasted with intense acidity and tart black-currant flavors.
title: Château Monbrison 2012 Barrel Sample  (Margaux)

description: Produced by the winemaking team of Château de Belcier in Castillon, this is a firmly structured, dry to the core wine. There are hints of ripe and juicy berry fruits to go with the dark tannins and dense texture. It needs several years to age.
title: Château de Monrecueil 2010  Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux

description: The rose-embossed bottle sets the scene for a soft, perfumed and fruity wine. It has attractive acidity as well as ripe red berries. With its spice and herbal edge, the wine is also textured, a contrast to the fine fruit. Drink now.
title: Château Roubine 2016 La Vie en Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: This impressive wine is full of potential. Powerful wood flavors are currently masking the fruitiness of the wine. However the weight of fruit and the rich texture promise good long-term aging.
title: Château Smith Haut Lafitte 2013  Pessac-Léognan

description: Hints of honeysuckle are very restrained on the nose. The palate is dry and shaped by rounded lemony freshness and hints of zesty spice. This is wonderfully light and deeply aromatic, but not obviously so. A wonderfully subtle expression of Muscat.
title: Allimant-Laugner 2015 Muscat (Alsace)

description: The Chenin Blanc shines here, with its smooth, creamy texture, hints of almonds, pears and an edge of firm texture. Acidity is balanced, a fine touch to finish a delicious wine.
title: Manoir de la Tête Rouge 2007 Chapitre 9  (Saumur)

description: This wine is ripe and packed with yellow-fruit flavors. It is smooth with a touch of butteriness. This is ready to drink, rich with bright acidity at the end.
title: Nadine et Maurice Guerrin 2014 La Côte Rôtie  (Saint-Véran)

description: This wood-matured wine features toast and spice as well as some pineapple and peach fruits. The wine is warm, rounded and soft, ready to drink now.
title: Cave de Lugny 2009 Les Beluses  (Mâcon-Chardonnay)

description: A blend of 80% Syrah and 20% Grenache, this boasts assertive aromas and flavors of ripe black cherry, raspberry, bramble and licorice root, with accents of black pepper and cocoa powder. The velvety tannins frame the dark fruit core on the palate, with lingering notes of dried fig and charred pepper on the close.
title: Château Tourril 2010 Panatella Syrah-Grenache (Minervois)

description: This wine is full, structured and still developing, with a bitter-chocolate edge that give a sharp character. This is balanced with warmer black-fruit flavors and acidity. The wine has great potential and needs another year. It will be best from the end of 2017.
title: Yves Leccia 2013 E Croce Red (Patrimonio)

description: The Cinsault that dominates this blend creates a light and gently fruity wine. It has attractive acidity and just the lightest of red-fruit flavors. The aftertaste is soft and tangy with orange zest.
title: Château de Brigue 2016 Les Meyans Elégance Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Still with its young tannins intact, the wine is firm and structured. It has spice, mint and black-currant fruits under the tight texture. It will still age so wait, if you want, for another year.
title: Château l'Espérance 2011  Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Soft Chardonnay, nicely balanced between ripe white fruits and some toast. It is an open, generous wine, enjoyable and ready to drink.
title: Antonin Rodet 2007 Bourgogne A. Rodet  (Bourgogne)

description: Ripe and juicy, this is perfumed, full of red berry flavor and attractively soft tannins. The wine feels broad and rich, ready to drink from 2015.
title: Château de Bel 2012 La Capitane  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: This seems a tad more restrained than usual for La Doriane, which has typically been extremely lush and exotic in the past. The 2012 isn't as over the top, but still shows plenty of ripe apricot and tropical fruit framed by smoky oak and baking spices. The finish is long and mouthwatering, suggesting at least a few years of longevity.
title: E. Guigal 2012 La Doriane  (Condrieu)

description: This wine brings together the two native grapes of Gaillac in one ripe, light and fruity whole. It has soft tannins and fine juicy acidity. There is a youthful rusticity to the wine and a crisp bright red-fruit aftertaste. Drink from 2018.
title: Lionel Osmin & Cie 2015 Duras-Braucol Red (Gaillac)

description: The initial impression of this wine is almost dry. That comes from the dense structure as well as the complex interplay between botrytis and acidity. Yellow fruits and bitter oranges show well, giving both acidity and final sweetness. Drink from 2018
title: Château Caillou 2011  Barsac

description: A pure impression ripe Golden Delicious apples shows on the nose of this wine, while the palate majors in citrus. Dry and fresh, it offers tingling zestiness with a pleasantly bitter edge. The dry finish lasts, leaving you to savor apple, zest and something less tangible—perhaps earth or stone. Drink now until 2030.
title: Domaine Ostertag 2014 Fronholz Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: The bitter-sweet edge of dark fir honey engulfs nose and palate which nonetheless has a core of bright, ripe, juicy and slightly sweet pear. The sweetness sings with that bitter tinge. This is rich but fresh and balanced and leaves with an off-dry aftertaste of more honey.
title: Domaine Fernand Engel 2013 Gloeckelberg Grand Cru Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: Soft, light and fruity, this wine is already approachable. It is an apple- and apricot-flavored Chardonnay, with just a tang of lemon zest that lends the finish some bite.
title: Antonin Rodet 2011 A. Rodet  (Bourgogne)

description: Spicy, high toast wood, the wine showing a burnt character at this stage. At the same time, there is good freshness, with green fruit characters. Give it 1–2 years.
title: Jacques Simonin 2008 Ammonites  (Pouilly-Fuissé)

description: The vines here have produced a 2009 that is hugely ripe, rounded and concentrated. With its sweet wood and vanilla flavors, with balancing apple and apricot fruits, it's a wine for pleasure soon, but it will also age.
title: La Chablisienne 2009 Château Grenouilles Grand Cru  (Chablis)

description: The natural honeyed character of Chenin Blanc shines through this simple, fruity wine. It has bright acidity and a creamed pear character that finishes with lime acidity. Screwcap.
title: Château Gaudrelle 2011 Le Petit Chenin No 11 Chenin Blanc (Vin de France)

description: The palest of pinks announces the lightness of touch that makes this wine so attractive. Sure, it has ripe strawberry flavors, but they are delicate, laced with acidity, good freshness. A real apéritif wine.
title: Deutz 2003 Rosé Brut  (Champagne)

description: Ripe and soft, here is a Chanson white that hasn't been overwhelmed by wood. Instead, the delicious yellow and white stone fruits come through, with a creamy edge reminiscent of buttered English muffins, finishing on a high note of juicy, thirst-quenching acidity.
title: Chanson Père et Fils 2005 Clos Saint-Jean Premier Cru  (Chassagne-Montrachet)

description: One of the small Sauvignon Blanc areas close to Sancerre, Menetou-Salon produces a light, crisp and herbal style of wine. This fits that bill well, its freshness contained within the flavors of hedgerow fruits and tangy acids.
title: Fournier Père et Fils 2010 Côtes de Morogues  (Menetou-Salon)

description: Intensely fruity, ripe and full-bodied. It has delicious apple and apricot flavors, bursting out of the glass with pure enjoyment. Lively acidity gives an extra dimension of brightness. Drink now.
title: Domaine FL 2010 Le Chenin Chenin Blanc (Anjou)

description: Classic ripe Muscadet, ripe but fresh, steely around the edges with delicious fresh fruits in the center. It is a lively wine, flavored with grapefruit and white currants, with an apple skin texture.
title: Domaine de la Grenaudière 2009 Sur Lie  (Muscadet Sèvre et Maine)

description: This is a lovely effort from a challenging vintage, offering up tarry notes that turn plummy and round with air. It's full-bodied without going over the top, with a long, concentrated finish framed by silky tannins. Drink now–2018.
title: La Bastide Saint Dominique 2008 Chloris Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: A rich, smooth wine, all ripe black fruit and soft tannins. It has weight and depth, but is ready to drink now.
title: Château Arnaucosse 2011  Bordeaux

description: This wine is smooth and ripe, with soft tannins that are already so well integrated. The texture is dense, full of blackberry fruits, full in the mouth and with a velvet final texture. Drink from 2017.
title: Château Lafleur-Gazin 2012  Pomerol

description: The blend of Colombard, Gros Manseng and Sauvignon Blanc gives a light mineral character. The wine is very fresh, very crisp and finely layered, with a depth of herbaceous and green fruit flavors. Screwcap.
title: Haut Marin 2010 Cuvée Marine White (Côtes de Gascogne)

description: Earthy, soft and ripe, this wine shows the richness of the vintage. It is full of red plum and red jelly flavors, and the tannins are dusty in the background. The acidity holds it all together, and the finish is juicy and fruity.
title: Jaffelin 2009  Volnay

description: While this wine is still young, it has great fruit and a good structure. Tense tannins lend form to ripe red fruits, with a touch of cigar-box aromas and a rich texture. This wine will show how well Chinon can age. Drink from 2018.
title: Domaine Grosbois 2015 Gabare  (Chinon)

description: This is perfumed and lemony, almost to the point of resembling furniture polish. It's pleasantly silky in texture, but the recurring notes of white pepper and citrus become somewhat one-dimensional and slightly bitter on the finish.
title: Vignobles de Balma Venitia 2011 Domaine du Paparotier  (Muscat de Beaumes de Venise)

description: A powerful wine, showing rich, opulent Chardonnay. The spice and acidity crispen the wine, but leave the powerful fruit in all its glory.
title: Domaine Leflaive 2007 Les Pucelles Premier Cru  (Puligny-Montrachet)

description: This wine has a caramel edge that gives richness but also detracts from the fruit. It offers a smooth texture along with a spicy edge. Drink now.
title: Château de Lisennes 2016 7 Hectares Vin Bio Rosé (Bordeaux Rosé)

description: This is a particularly fruity rosé that's not quite dry but very fresh and full of strawberry flavors. It is fresh, ripe and ready to drink even in the winter.
title: Jean-Baptiste Adam NV Brut Rosé Sparkling (Crémant d'Alsace)

description: This ripe, concentrated wine is full of tannins and ripe blackberry fruit. It is still youthfully dry, but shows the potential to develop for 3–5 years.
title: Château la Chapelle Maillard 2011  Bordeaux

description: This blend of 50-50 Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon is lightly herbal, with a touch of attractive tannins. It's ripe but preserves a crisp edge to balance the caramel flavor. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Château Carignan 2016 L'Orangerie de Carignan Rosé (Bordeaux Rosé)

description: While Colombo's Les Abeilles is a more traditional Rhône blend, this offering marries 70% Viognier with 30% Roussanne. The result is a medium-bodied wine hinting at honeyed peaches and tinged with anise that seems to be on a fast-developing course. Drink it over the next several months.
title: Jean-Luc Colombo 2008 La Redonne White (Côtes du Rhône)

description: While this wine has high acidity, it also offers attractive fruitiness. It is light and bright, with a layer of spice and pepper. Drink the wine now.
title: Château d'Haurets 2016 Rosé (Bordeaux Rosé)

description: The second wine of Ausone is powerful with plenty of firm tannins. At the same time, it is ripe, with softness from the fruit and light extraction. It is a very fine wine, delicious with its acidity and its black-currant fruitiness. Drink this wine from 2024.
title: Château Ausone 2014 La Chapelle d'Ausone  (Saint-Émilion)

description: A concentrated, hugely rich wine with lively caramel and toast aromas, followed by ripe fruits. It is generous and open, with apricots and nectarines giving richness, while kiwi gives the green, crisp character. The aftertaste is all minerality. Screwcap.
title: Domaine Laroche 2005 Les Blanchots Grand Cru  (Chablis)

description: The wine is soft and ripe. Generous blackberry fruits and a smooth texture from wood aging give this wine its richness and open character. Hints of vanilla and spice add to the perfumed aftertaste. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Amour 2014  Médoc

description: Young and herbaceous, it has lemon, peach and tight acidity that give a fresh, fruity feel. It's lively, ready to drink now.
title: Yvon Mau 2012 Grand Palais  (Bordeaux Blanc)

description: The wine is soft, fruity and immediately accessible. Red-berry fruit and balanced acidity are paired with red cherry and soft tannins. The wine is juicy and will be ready to drink from late 2017.
title: L. Tramier & Fils 2015 Collection Premier Cru  (Santenay)

description: Produced from vines that are up to 80-years-old and now organic, this concentrated wood-aged wine is perfumed, rich and firmly structured. Dark tannins line the wine supporting the black plum and cherry fruits and acidity. Richard Rottiers, new on the scene in 2007, created a cru that is going to be a very fine wine. Drink from 2018.
title: Domaine Richard Rottiers 2015  Moulin-à-Vent

description: An attractively soft and fruity wine, flavored with red cherries and given a lift with acidity. It's a simple, lightly structured wine that can be drunk now.
title: Domaine Pfister 2012 Pinot Noir (Alsace)

description: Light scents of fresh grass, white flowers, waxy melon and sweet orange dance in the bouquet of this easy-drinking white. Those notes continue through to the fruity palate, with a pleasant roundness to the midpalate. Drink now.
title: Wild Pig 2016 Viognier (Pays d'Oc)

description: Olive and espresso notes are backed by bright cherry fruit in this medium-bodied Syrah. It's a pleasant quaff that turns slightly dry on the finish; try it after 2011.
title: Paul Jaboulet Aîné 2005 Le Grand Pompée  (Saint-Joseph)

description: Touches of toast from the wood aging give a spicy character. The toast brings out acidity at this stage rather than sweetness, with lemon and lime flavors dominant over the honey and botrytis in the background. Drink from 2018.
title: Château Broustet 2011  Barsac

description: Dark chocolate and rich plum sauce add complexity to this black fruit-driven Merlot. Blackberry preserves and soft plum guts fill the mouth, with a touch of green pepper appearing on the finish. Medium weight with a creamy texture and a medium-length finish. Drink now.
title: Domaines Astruc 2009 Reserve Merlot (Vin de Pays d'Oc)

description: This is a youthful, fruity wine with crisp yellow-fruit and citrus flavors, with a toastiness from wood aging. Sourced from Saint-Aubin, it has a cool, fresh feel that complements its pristine fruitiness. It's for drinking soon, but will also improve with age through 2021.
title: Jean-Claude Boisset 2011 En Remilly Premier Cru  (Saint-Aubin)

description: This wood-laden wine, spicy and toasty, also offers plenty of fruit weight, giving richness to the palate. Fresh black currants carry through to finish the wine with a lift.
title: Château Baracan 2010  Cadillac Côtes de Bordeaux

description: This is a smooth, yellow fruit-flavored wine that comes from a small area of the appellation. It has ripe apricot fruit and a toasty, creamy texture, giving it rich, full flavor. A light touch of textured minerality lifts the wine on the finish.
title: Domaine de la Denante 2015 Les Maillettes  (Saint-Véran)

description: This wine melds firm tannins with fruit that is ripe, concnetrated and juicy. Brisk acidity brings the elements together, producing a wine that is full bodied and with good potential. Drink now and until 2020.
title: Château Calissanne 2009 Clos Victoire Cabernet-Syrah (Coteaux d'Aix-en-Provence)

description: Lightly perfumed and floral, this is classic mineral and gunflint Riesling. It is light in texture, clean and refreshing, with some crisp pear fruit and green plum skin tannins.
title: Dopff & Irion 2006 Riesling (Alsace)

description: Ripe red fruits are the main character of this soft, rounded wine. Attractive acidity with grapefruit and lemon zest flavors are balanced by a warmer element.
title: Domaine Sainte Lucie 2013 MIP Made in Provence Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Powerful, with tannins that are seamlessly woven into the fruitiness, this is a rounded, ripe and concentrated wine. The berry fruits and the juicy damsons are as important as the generous, soft texture. The wine will develop over the medium-term; drink from 2017.
title: Manuel Olivier 2011  Clos de Vougeot

description: Crisp and elegant in feel, this has apricot and peach flavors that are balanced by the tight structure and the green-fruit-like acidity. The layers of toast have integrated with the ebullient fruit character and the taut, minerally finish. For aging 3–4 years.
title: Olivier Leflaive 2010 Dents du Chien Premier Cru  (Saint-Aubin)

description: Deep pink in color, this is a caramel-flavored, heavy wine. It is fat, very full and bitter at the end.
title: Château Queyssard 2014 Rosé (Bordeaux Rosé)

description: Dominated by its 86% Merlot, this is a fruity wine that is surprisingly light for the vintage. It comes from the plain between Saint-Émilion and the Dordogne River, giving a soft style with just a touch of tannin. Drink from 2017.
title: Château Joly 2014  Saint-Émilion

description: The wine is bright and fruity. It has crisp acidity and a light touch of citrus to go with the lively acidity. Its blend brings out the herbal flavor of Colombard with just a touch of honey from Gros Manseng. Drink now.
title: Domaine la Taste 2015 Colombard-Ugni Blanc-Gros Manseng-Sauvignon White (Côtes de Gascogne)

description: A certain creamy nuttiness is suggested on the nose. The sunniness of 2015 is expressed by a generous mirabelle ripeness. The body still is very slight and subtle and all about a graceful texture. There's a lovely touch of lemon freshness on the finish.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2015 Trois Châteaux 'E' Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Hints of smoky bacon and wood are now giving this wine some maturity. It has firm tannins still, but also a rich texture, with some red berries still present.
title: Château de la Terrière 2008 Cuvée de la Lure  (Moulin-à-Vent)

description: Light structure and perfumes come from the blending of Syrah with Muscat. It's an intriguing wine, providing spice as well as fresh fruits and acidity. Crisp and fruity at the end.
title: Domaine Terre de Mistral 2013 Mireille Rosé (Mediterranée)

description: Ripe aromas of black plum, cherry and boysenberry are at the forefront of this richly fruited wine. The wine's lushness is complemented by hints of pepper and baking spice, as well as medium tannins that lend a subtle structure to the palate.
title: La Châsse 2015 Pinot Noir (Vin de France)

description: A firm wine from a cool vineyard. It is structured, dry and solid, the fruit dominated by tannins. There is an austere character here that obviously needs time to fill out. Give it five years.
title: Bouchard Père & Fils 2008 Les Cailles Premier Cru  (Nuits-St.-Georges)

description: Structured, concentrated, a wine that shows power from both fruit and tannins. The opulent strawberry flavors are paralleled by firm, dry tannins. As the combination comes together, this will become a glorious wine.
title: Joseph Drouhin 2008  Charmes-Chambertin

description: Lovely notes of Reineclaude plums play on the nose. The palate is soft but also has echoes of pebble and stone. Fluid and fresh, the wine even shows overtones of white blossom on the palate. It's a lovely, lasting and authentic expression of Alsace.
title: Josmeyer 2014 H Vieilles Vignes Pinot Auxerrois (Alsace)

description: This shows a more serious side to Gamay. Black cherry and plum fruits are sustained by firm tannins and tight, mineral acidity. It needs a few months to develop. Drink from 2015.
title: Vignerons de Bel Air 2013 Eté Fleuri  (Chiroubles)

description: Boldly tannic, this structured, wood-aged wine is just starting out. It's as much dry wood and tannins as fruit at this stage, although the fruit and mineral texture do promise aging. There is significant acidity at the end. Drink from 2016.
title: Domaine de Leyre-Loup 2012 Réserve Louis Leyre-Loup  (Morgon)

description: The second wine of Léoville las Cases has structure and richness with plenty of concentration. At the same time, it has so much fresh, ripe fruit, laced with intense acidity that it is more fresh then structured. With this balance already established it will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Château Léoville Las Cases 2013 Le Petit Lion  (Saint-Julien)

description: The wine is full of fresh fruits and crisp acidity. Alongside that, ripe plum skins and a mineral texture promise short-term aging. The old vines give concentration that adds a firm structure at the end.
title: Les Frères Perroud 2013 Vieilles Vignes  (Brouilly)

description: From one of a group of chateaus owned by the Guyon family of Rolland de By, this is an intensely fruity wine. It has finely balanced tannins and acidity along with smoky flavors and layers of delicious black currant fruit. Drink from 2019.
title: Château La Clare 2014  Médoc

description: A lifted, phenolic note of Bosc pear peel suggests both freshness and maturity. The soft, rounded palate sings with pear sweetness and finishes medium-dry. There is softness and pliability, but a guiding, focused freshness that provides excellent balance.
title: Domaine Moltès 2014 Steinert Grand Cru Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: A classic rosé blend of Grenache and Cinsault, this is soft, crisp and fruity. The smooth texture gains attractive lift from acidity and raspberry fruits. It is light and bright at the end.
title: Château Pas du Cerf 2014 Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: 89-91 Barrel sample. Caramel aromas, a wine that seems over-extracted, tough. The fruit has a juicy element, but misses out on elegance.
title: Château Phélan-Ségur 2009 Barrel sample  (Saint-Estèphe)

description: A beautifully balanced wine, mixing an intense, nervy, crisp fruit character with light wood flavors and the most piercing acidity. This is a serious wine, for aging over 3–5 years while retaining impeccable fruit.
title: William Fèvre 2008 Fourchaume Premier Cru  (Chablis)

description: A sunny touch of peach adds a very fruity edge to the strawberry nose of this vivid, pale-pink sparkler. The frothy mousse is very lively and makes this utterly refreshing, highlighting notes of red apples. The finish is dry and clean-cut.
title: Cave de Cleebourg NV Clérostein Pinot Noir Sparkling (Crémant d'Alsace)

description: From old vines and only produced in top vintages, this ripe perfumed wine is impressive. Rich with tropical fruits, layers of vanilla and acidity, it is full, up-front and packed with concentrated flavors and textures. Like many wines from this appellation, it can age and will be better from 2019.
title: Domaine Chatelain 2015 Prestige  (Pouilly-Fumé)

description: Very supple wine, with great richness and density. It is all so complete, a pleasure, powerful yet also with sweet opulent fruits layered with dark tannins. For long-term aging.
title: Château Branaire-Ducru 2009  Saint-Julien

description: This wine is crisp with acidity but rounded on the palate with concentrated pink-grapefruit flavors. It has a rich, fruity, juicy aftertaste.
title: Château Ballan-Larquette 2012  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Despite coming from a universally hailed vintage, this generic Bordeaux is still weedy, herbal and lean. But it also has decent cassis and cherry fruit, some tasty vanilla and cinnamon notes from oak aging, and a tart, dry finish. It's light, but it hasn't been dumbed down for the mass market.
title: Malesan 2000 Rouge  (Bordeaux)

description: A firm, ripe wine, with good sweet black currant fruit flavors. It is structured, but mainly showing fruit rather than tannins. There is a fine, fresh finish, hinting at wood.
title: Xavier Flouret 2005 Château Haut-Meneau La Victoire  (Premieres Côtes de Blaye)

description: Produced from the young vines of Château de Fuissé, this wine is soft and rounded with just enough acidity to go with the sweet peach flavors. It's a gentle wine, ready to drink now.
title: Vincent 2009 Marie-Antoinette  (Pouilly-Fuissé)

description: This is a structured wine, almost tannic in character. It has a rich, textured character that will allow the wine well. Apple and pear flavors are dominant along with the spice from wood aging. Drink from 2020.
designation: Domaine du Pavillon Les Charmes Premier Cru
title: Albert Bichot 2014 Domaine du Pavillon Les Charmes Premier Cru  (Meursault)

description: The estate is under the same ownership as Château la Dominique in Saint-Émilion. This dense wine balances acidity with intense fruitiness. The tannins are now softening, giving a rich structure plus a concentrated leather and spice character. Drink from 2016.
title: Château Clément-Pichon 2010  Haut-Médoc

description: For the vintage, which generally provided rather ripe fruit, this is a bit tight and stiffly tannic. The cherry cobbler aromas and flavors are certainly pleasurable, but finish crisp and firm. Give it a few years in the cellar, and drink 2017–2023.
title: Louis Bernard 2012 Les Carbonnières  (Gigondas)

description: A nice nose of black and blue berries with a hint of leather opens this value Syrah. Well built, it shows a bit of chocolate and decent length. There’s just a touch of green herb in there and it closes dry with fairly full tannins.
title: Calvet 1998 Syrah (Vin de Pays d'Oc)

description: A soft, gently fruity wine, it has warm tannins, layers of smooth vanilla from wood aging and a finish that brings out the acidity of the vintage. Fresh, fruity and ready to drink.
title: Château de Sainte-Gemme 2011  Haut-Médoc

description: This simple wine is full of fruit. There is no wood aging here, just ripe pear and apricot flavors that are gently textured. The wine, with its crisp final acidity, is ready to drink.
title: Ropiteau 2015 Les Plants Nobles Chardonnay (Vin de France)

description: Dry and firm, this is smothered with hard-edged tannins that give the wine a tough character. It will soften a little bit, but not completely.
title: Horeau-Beylot 2009 Château Grand Martinet  (Saint-Émilion)

description: This full-bodied, creamy Crozes-Hermitage far outpaces expectations for the appellation, combining cherry-berry fruit with cracked pepper, mocha and licorice. Then it finishes long, anchored by a marriage of fruit and spice. It's ready to drink now, but should evolve positively through at least 2017.
title: Maison Nicolas Perrin 2009  Crozes-Hermitage

description: Ripe Sauvignon Blanc, bringing out pear and apple flavors, topped with cream. There is a bite from an apple skin texture, along with bright, delicate acidity.
title: Guy Saget 2008 La Petite Perrière Sauvignon Blanc (Vin de Pays de France)

description: Slightly bruised apple on nose and palate also becomes apparent on the textured, balanced palate of this wine that aligns citric freshness with more mellow and sonorous tones of ripeness. The finish is dry and whistle-clean.
title: Lucien Albrecht 2015 Réserve Riesling (Alsace)

description: Dry, firm and tannic, this is a wine with great potential. It has weight, ripe berry fruits and a solid character. Wood aging shows lightly, allowing the ripeness of the fruit to dominate. Drink from 2016.
title: Château des Demoiselles 2011  Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Les Cimels is a solid, entry-level Costières de Nîmes blend of Syrah, Carignan and Grenache. Blackberry fruit dominates this full-bodied red, picking up intriguing tarry notes and glancing those with crisp, citrusy acids. Long and mouthwatering on the finish.
title: Château d'Or et de Gueules 2012 Les Cimels Red (Costières de Nîmes)

description: Rounded, almost luscious fruitiness is at the core of this easy wine. Lychee, lemon, peach and apricot scented with rose petals on a rich palate close with orange peel freshness.
title: Jean-Marc Bernhard 2014 Vieilles Vignes Gewurztraminer (Alsace)

description: An off-dry wine, nuts and pear flavors, laced with almonds. The wine is soft, with hints of red currants and a gentle, ripe aftertaste. Screwcap.
title: Pierre Chainier 2009 Roc de Châteauvieux  (Vouvray)

description: This nine-acre property has produced a fruity wine that has only the lightest tannins. It is much more to do with red cherries, red currants and crisp acidity. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2015 Domaine de la Martingale  (Brouilly)

description: High, bright notes of the ripest pears pervade everything. They are edged with the slightly bitter tinge of fir honey which counters the sweetness of the fruit. This is balanced, off-dry and finishes with pleasant warmth.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2013 Eichberg Grand Cru Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: This vineyard is regarded as the finest of the Grand Crus, certainly for its more serious character. This 2014 has great complexity, toast and spice mingling with the mineral and white-fruit flavors. It is obviously set for long-term aging with its wood still not fully integrated. Drink this impressive wine from 2020.
title: Jean-Marc Brocard 2014 Les Clos Grand Cru  (Chablis)

description: There's a faint coppery tinge to this wine's rosy pink hue, and the fruit is somewhat muted, but this remains an enjoyable drink. Leather and scorched cherry flavors are carried by a supple, rounded mouthfeel, finishing with moderate length and intensity. Easy to drink, but not one to hold more than another few months. Certified biodynamic.
title: Christian Leperchois 2012 Lunar Apogé  (Tavel)

description: Packed with fruit and spice, this is a great food wine. It is full bodied, rich with raspberry flavors gathered into a structure of light tannins and edgy minerality. The aftertaste brings out a crisper character.
title: Les Vins Bréban 2015 Mimi en Provence Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: A blend of 65% Sauvignon Blanc and 35% Sémillon, this is a crisp fruity wine with attractive acidity and lemon flavors that have been given some time to soften out. It has a touch of barrel aging, but the wine is really all about fruitiness. Drink now.
title: Château de Birot 2014  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Subdued earth notes precede the ripe freshness of red apple and mirabelle. A touch of residual sweetness boosts ripe fruit flavors of pear and orange, red apple and mirabelle plum. Lemon freshness brightens the palate and makes for an appetizing, zingy and refreshing finish.
title: Domaine Gresser 2015 Duttenberg Riesling (Alsace)

description: This is firm and tannic, reflecting the challenges of the vintage, yet it does show classic garrigue aromas and flavors of black olives and dark cherries. Drink it now with grilled meats, or hold it a year or two in the hopes of it softening a little.
title: Famille Perrin 2013 Red (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

description: Less bright and perfumed than La Coterie's Passe Colline bottling, this is still a soft, approachable wine with ample cherry fruit. Hints of leather and white pepper give it a bit of complexity. The blend is 65% Grenache, 25% Syrah and 10% Carignan.
title: Vignobles La Coterie 2010 Pierre du Diable Red (Ventoux)

description: This is a crisp and fruity style of Champagne, lightweight and attractively fresh. It comes from the original of the three Champagne producers with Heidsieck in their name, and this nonvintage seems to be finding its form after several fallow years. Drink this apple-fruited wine now.
title: Heidsieck & Co Monopole NV Blue Top Brut  (Champagne)

description: This is a dense, flavorful and powerfully structured wine, brimming with notes of black cherry, plum, currant, baking spice, black pepper, crushed violet and bittersweet chocolate. It's full and chewy on the palate, with a firm structure and gripping tannins. Hints of licorice root, mocha and toasted sweet spice unfold on the finish. It's delicious now, but it should hold well through 2020.
title: Gérard Bertrand 2011 Le Viala Red (Minervois La Liviniere)

description: From vines in the Entre-deux-Mers, this is a cool wine although with fresh black-currant fruits and balanced tannins. Dominated by Merlot blended with Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc, it has a juicy character that leaves a fresh taste as well as lively acidity. Drink from the end of 2016.
title: Château Vrai Caillou 2013 La Fleur de Ribeyrolles  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: 92-94 Very structured wine, its fruit tannins dominant. It has power and extreme concentration. Dark and dense.
title: Château Siran 2010 Barrel sample  (Margaux)

description: With its relatively high alcohol, it is logical that this should be a rich wine. It has juicy blackberry fruits cut with acidity while having weight and density. It is almost pure Merlot which gives the wine its fruit and concentration. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Lestage Simon 2014  Haut-Médoc

description: For a white Bordeaux, this is hugely rich and ripe. With its spice and toast, the wood-aging of the Muscadelle has added extra complexity. The alcohol gives the wine a peppery character that is less attractive while also removing freshness. Consider this wine a curiosity.
title: Château de Bel 2014  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Importer Bobby Kacher has a seemingly limitless portfolio of bargains from the south of France, and this one boasts an intriguing nose of pear, honey and star anise. It's more focused and crisp than many wines from this region, turning citrusy on the finish. Drink now-2012.
title: Domaine de la Petite Cassagne 2008 White (Costières de Nîmes)

description: A structured and concentrated wine that is packed with tannins. It does have some black fruit under this dry structure although the wine misses out on richness. It will be ready to drink from 2017.
designation: Réserve Louis Leyre-Loup Le Regard de l'Ange
title: Domaine de Leyre-Loup 2013 Réserve Louis Leyre-Loup Le Regard de l'Ange  (Morgon)

description: From one of the Leflaive family vineyards close to Puligny-Montrachet, this is as rich as Aligoté can get. Even so, the texture is crisp, with fresh kiwi and herbs, finishing with intense acidity.
title: Olivier Leflaive 2009 Aligoté du Domaine  (Bourgogne Aligoté)

description: A fresh and crisp wine, it is softly fruity with intense layers of acidity. It's immediately appealing with red fruits and crisp citrus at the end.
title: Domaine Saint-André de Figuière 2013 Cuvée Signature Magali Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: This is Barton & Guestier's home vineyard and, in 2006, it has produced a finely crafted wine. The balance is all there, with ripe red berry fruits and cranberry juice adding to the firm, dark core of tannins. It is worth aging  for two to three years but is certainly drinkable now.
title: Château Magnol 2006  Haut-Médoc

description: Soft and ripe, this young wine is already to drink. Light tannins and a touch of spice give it just the right amount of richness from this good vintage. The wine is filled with red berries and is ready to drink.
title: Les Vignerons Réunis de Monségur 2015 Belle Prairie  (Bordeaux)

description: 93-95 Barrel sample. Very rich and dense, solid, chunky textured wine. The tannins are severe, very firm, suggesting a wine with considerable power.
title: Château d'Armailhac 2009 Barrel sample  (Pauillac)

description: Ripe and fruity, this is a simple, easy wine with red berry fruits and soft tannins. It has lively fruitiness that is enjoyable now.
title: Château Vieil Orme 2011  Bordeaux

description: Rich and spicy, this 90% Merlot and 10% Cabernet Sauvignon from 10 acres generous and ripe, with warm tannins and a fruity, red berry character. This blend has attractive acidity and spicy flavors from the combination of wood and stainless-steel aging. This is only the second vintage from enologist Jean-Rémi Larrat's own property. It is worth aging, so drink from 2017.
title: Château Chatard 2012  Cadillac Côtes de Bordeaux

description: The aromas are rather shy on the nose—there is just a mere floral whiff. The palate is dry and lets us see subtle varietal aromas: golden peach, a touch of honeysuckle spice, a hint of jasmine, aromatic orange zest. This is a restrained, streamlined and elegant varietal expression. Drink now through 2030.
title: Trimbach 2014 Gewurztraminer (Alsace)

description: The tannins are still intense in this dark, structured wine. It does have red fruits although there are also hints of green and stalky flavors that are less attractive. The wine will always be dry although the red fruits could push further forward. Try waiting until 2017.
title: Château Carpe Diem 2013 Major Red (Côtes de Provence)

description: Light, fresh and fruity, this is an immediately attractive wine. Apple and citrus dominate with a tang of orange zest to add an extra refreshing character. Not for aging, ready to drink now.
title: Cave des Grands Crus Blancs 2012 Vieilles Vignes  (Saint-Véran)

description: A rich, wood-aged wine, its high proportion of Sémillon gives it a full and creamy character. Apples mingle with white plums, spicy quince and fresh rhubarb. Already delicious, it should age. Drink from 2016.
title: Château Brown 2011  Pessac-Léognan

description: Powerful, ripe fruit, now beginning to open up, is the dominant character of this wine. It's full in the mouth, packed with black plums and ripe damsons and finished with soft tannins and a slight warmth.
title: Château Carignan 2009  Cadillac Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Full, rich, quite heavy in style, this is only just brut, with its crushed strawberry flavor and low acidity. It works, as a fine wine for a red-fruit dessert, the balance remaining right up to the very soft aftertaste.
title: Perrier Jouët NV Blason Brut Rosé  (Champagne)

description: This medium-bodied blend of Grenache, Syrah and Mourvèdre shows off delicate floral and herbal notes laced over a core of raspberry compote. It's slightly creamy in texture, showing supple tannins on the finish, where it picks up a hint of bay leaf. Ready to drink now.
title: Château de Nages 2012 Buti Nages Red (Costières de Nîmes)

description: This attractive herbal and spicy wine is the entry-level wine at this showcase estate. The wine is rich, generous with a character that reveals both crisp fruit and ripeness. That makes it rich and very drinkable young.
title: Château de Tracy 2016 Madamoiselle de T  (Pouilly-Fumé)

description: A ripe, soft, generous wine, laden with blackberry fruits as well as spice from wood aging. Rich and full-bodied, it has power and concentration from dark tannins as well as the ripe, opulent black fruits of the vintage. Drink from 2014.
title: Château Siaurac 2009  Lalande de Pomerol

description: A ripe, juicy and forward wine, this is all fruit—but it's delicious fruit. The tannins are restrained behind the exuberant fruitiness. It may not be classic Bordeaux, but it is likely to be very drinkable in 2–3 years.
title: Château Mongravey 2010  Margaux

description: This is a ripe, fruity wine with its black-currant fruit and balanced sweet tannins. It has a fine elegant texture and juicy acidity. All these elements promise a wine that will be delicious to drink from 2019. The daughters of owners Bernadette Faure and Alain Aubert oversee vineyards, winemaking and marketing.
title: Château Hyot 2014  Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux

description: This rich wine is ripe with red-berry fruits, mango and peach. Full in the mouth, it has a touch of spice from the Mourvèdre in the blend. Crisp and lively, the wine could age until 2018, although it will be best from late 2016.
title: Les Vignobles Gueissard 2015 Cuvée G Rosé (Bandol)

description: Bringing out all the minerality of the chalk soils, this rounded wine is also packed with rich fruits. The wine is just approaching its peak, showing wonderful lemon and white stone fruits.
title: Pol Roger 2000 Blanc de Blancs Brut Chardonnay (Champagne)

description: Blending Pinot Noir and Gamay, this wine comes from a new Burgundy-wide appellation that can include fruit from Beaujolais. It's a style of wine that needs to be drunk young and fruity, with black cherry flavors and refreshing acidity. Drink this lively wine now.
title: Louis Max 2014  Coteaux Bourguignons

description: This is a fresh, strawberry-flavored wine with a crisp, dry aftertaste. An attractive tangy character gives life and brilliance. Ready to drink, it is a real apéritif wine.
title: Château Grand Boise 2014 Rosé (Côtes de Provence Sainte-Victoire)

description: Nose and palate are full of juicy, rose-scented plums. A good dose of sweetness boosts those flavors even more, but fails to create overall balance. This is rather rich and full bodied.
title: Domaine Jean-Luc Mader 2014 Gewurztraminer (Alsace)

description: Packed with juicy red cherries, this is a fresh, fruity Beaujolais to drink this winter. There is just a touch of tannin as well as refreshing acidity.
title: Domaine Pral 2009 Cuvée Terroir  (Beaujolais)

description: Earthy, herbal accents of forest floor and garrigue complement the black-fruit core of this solid, ripe wine. The palate is juicy and rich, and although it's a bit straightforward, it's balanced, attractive and enjoyable nonetheless.
title: Bernard Magrez 2011 Si Mon Père Savait... Red (Côtes du Roussillon)

description: The promise of aromatic, ripe stone fruit is immediately evident: vine-ripened peach and baked apple are lush and perfumed. The palate is equally fruitful, but tempered by dryness and a stony, taut and structured background. This is clean and poised and is ready now but will develop beautifully until 2020.
title: Domaine Jean Sipp 2012 Kirchberg de Ribeauvillé Grand Cru Riesling (Alsace)

description: With its stalky edge and red fruits, this crisp red wine has acidity, some tight tannins and a juicy aftertaste. It is ready to drink young, possibly slightly chilled.
title: Jean Perrier et Fils 2015 Cuvée Gastronomie Mondeuse (Savoie)

description: Offering uncomplicated, easy drinking, this Grenache-Syrah blend is lush and immediately accessible, displaying the charm of the 2011 vintage. Drink it over the next year or two for its gulpable cherry and chocolate flavors.
title: Les Trois Couronnes 2011 Red (Côtes du Rhône)

description: Light, fresh, slightly grassy but more in the white fruit flavor profile. It's soft, with a citrus twang to it, great to drink as an apéritif, leaving a soft, creamy aftertaste.
title: Caves Fleury 2006 Sauvignon Blanc  (Bordeaux Blanc)

description: From vineyards on the cooler south bank of the Serein, this wine is crisp and tightly coiled despite the warmth of the 2015 vintage. It's built on a backbone of sturdy minerality, fleshed out with crisp, citrus fruit, and will be ready to drink from late 2017.
title: Domaine Christian Moreau Père et Fils 2015  Chablis
winery: Domaine Christian Moreau Père et Fils

description: Light to medium in body, this rosé is crisper and tauter than many coming out of the Southern Rhône. Zest citrus aromas lead into flavors of grapefruit, with crushed stone notes reverberating on the finish. Drink now.
title: Domaine de Mourchon 2016 Loubié Rosé

description: With its bold, jammy blackberries and a hint of black pepper on the nose, this wine is all about delivering a flavor punch. Straightforward berry fruit is tinged with licorice in a bulky but satisfying package.
title: Château Val Joanis 1998 Estate Bottled Red (Côtes du Luberon)

description: Smooth and balanced, this wine brings together generous red fruits and fresh acidity. Underneath, attractive, dry tannins give shape to this otherwise ready-to-drink, fruity wine. It's is a generous blend of 60% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Sauvignon, 10% Cabernet Franc and 5% Malbec.
title: Closerie du Bailli 2014 Tradition  (Côtes de Bourg)

description: Big and bold, this lives up to the reputation of richer Meursault. Yet crispness, a core of tight structure, toast and fresh fruits are always present. The play between the characters is delicious and likely to allow the wine to age over several years.
title: Bouchard Père & Fils 2007 Genevrières Premier Cru  (Meursault)

description: Honeyed but otherwise not overly aromatic, this full-bodied, corpulent Condrieu still provides enjoyable drinking. The weighty notes of honeyed citrus linger on the finish, but drink up over the next six months or so.
title: Les Vins de Vienne 2009 Les Archevêques  (Condrieu)

description: Already mature, this is a full-bodied, perfumed wine full of stone-fruit and toast notes. It has a bitter almond character that gives the wine an edginess while spice lends a peppery sensation to the palate. Drink now before it fades.
title: Frédéric Mallo 2009 Réserve Spéciale Riesling (Alsace)

description: This wine is soft, with a light fruitiness and earthy flavors typical of some Pinot Noir. The tannic element gives a dry core to this simple, fruity wine. Drink now.
title: Jean-Luc and Paul Aegerter 2012  Bourgogne

description: 95–97. Barrel sample. This is a very solid wine with tannins that are very firm and dense. There is power without excessive alcohol, lending a classic feel, and a pure line from fruit, acidity and tannin. The result is as much about fruit as structure.
title: Château Léoville Las Cases 2012 Barrel Sample  (Saint-Julien)

description: Touches of toast and tannin give enough structure. The red berry fruits taste firm and extracted, giving a bitter edge. It's a wine that brings out the structured character of Pinot Noir rather than its fruit.
title: Chavet Fils 2012 Cuvée Exception  (Menetou-Salon)

description: Concentrated and very full in the mouth, the wine's acidity is tight, wrapped around lemon and grapefruit characters. Good depth of flavor in this intense wine.
title: Gadais Père et Fils 2009 Vieilles Vignes Sur Lie  (Muscadet Sèvre et Maine)

description: Very textured wine, with apple skin tautness, followed by acidity, a touch of wood, and final spice. Attractive wine that could age for 1–2 years.
title: Marcel Couturier 2008 Aux Scelles  (Pouilly-Fuissé)

description: The name is spot-on for a wine with a pink-pearl color. It is gently fruity with soft red fruits and a touch of pepper. The aftertaste is crisp, clean and refreshing.
title: Château Réal Martin 2015 Perle du Rosé Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: With its tough tannins, this solid wine also shows dark fruits and black plum skins, finishing with fresher acidity.
title: Château Coutelin Merville 2011  Saint-Estèphe

description: Plan de Dieu is a broad, stony plain near Gigondas that's an underrated commune. This is a firmly built, medium-bodied wine, with lifted, floral aromas and bright cherry and raspberry fruit. chocolate notes emerge on the finish, which simultaneously shows some dusty tannins and mouthwatering acids. Drink now–2022.
designation: Domaine du Père Hugues Plan de Dieu
title: Louis Bernard 2015 Domaine du Père Hugues Plan de Dieu Red (Côtes du Rhône Villages)

description: Richly textured wine that oozes jammy red fruits and ripe tannins. It has a good dry core followed by sweet acidity and a juicy berry character. The wine has some power and aging potential.
title: Château de la Crée 2008 Clos du Château  (Santenay)

description: Based in the village of Cramant, Voirin-Jumel has access to Grand Cru Chardonnay vineyards. This crisp and tight wine is packed with acidity, fresh citrus and a taut, steely texture. It is still young and fruity, and could do with some bottle age.
designation: Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs Brut
title: Voirin-Jumel NV Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs Brut Chardonnay (Champagne)

description: Scented impressions of ripe pear and aloe vera pervade this wine. The palate shows texture and more fruit than you might expect from slender Sylvaner. The finish is brisk, clean and taut.
title: Boeckel 2015 Vieilles Vignes Sylvaner (Alsace)

description: Following the latest Provence fashion in having a glass-stoppered bottle, this wine is the top of the range for this producer. It is soft and ripe to start but deliciously balanced and crisp at the end. Named after Madeleine or Mado, an imaginary Provencal lady, the wine is ready to drink.
title: Les Vins Bréban 2016 Mado en Provence Rosé (Côtes de Provence)
